instruction: Send an email to a public park office asking about picnic reservations. The email must include the adjective "available" in the sense of "able to be used or obtained" and the verb "confirm" in the sense of "establish the truth or correctness of something." Begin the email with the phrase "Dear Park Office," and conclude it with the phrase "Looking forward to your response." The email should contain exactly 150 words, with each sentence containing no more than 20 words. Write the email exclusively in English, incorporating a relevant English idiom. Tailor the email for a general audience, avoiding technical jargon, and provide a brief explanation of why the reservation is needed, including the intended date, number of attendees, and any specific requirements. Include a relevant Chinese idiom in the email, presented in Chinese.
response:
Dear Park Office,

I hope this message finds you well today. We wish to inquire about picnic table availability soon. Many families need these spots for summer gatherings. Please confirm if any tables remain open for booking. We plan to host twelve guests on July twenty-first. Children will need extra space for safe playtime. We also require trash bins for easy cleanup later. This event aims to bring our community together closely. It is time to secure our spot before it slips away. We believe the park is a true gem for us. 万事胜意 (Everything goes well) is our wish for this trip. Kindly let us know how to proceed with the request. Your prompt reply would be greatly appreciated by all. Thank you very much for your time and effort here. Looking forward to your response.

Conflicts between Format Constraints.new_constraint: Include a relevant Chinese idiom in the email, presented in Chinese.
Conflicts between Format Constraints.org_constraint: Write the email exclusively in English, incorporating a relevant English idiom.
